content:
Imagine you and your friends want to keep track of who has which toys, but there’s no teacher or adult to help. Instead, everyone writes down every time someone trades a toy in their own special notebook. Before writing it down, all friends check with each other to make sure the trade really happened. This way, everyone agrees on the trades, and no one can cheat or say they have a toy they don’t.

That’s a bit like how blockchain works! It’s a big list of trades (or transactions) that lots of people keep copies of. Instead of one bank keeping track, lots of computers around the world check and save the information together. This makes it super hard for anyone to cheat because everyone’s notebooks must match. So, blockchain helps people trust each other, even without a bank or boss watching!
